name: Tom Steyer
subject: I’m asking you
body:
| | | |
---
---
Friend — I am so incredibly proud of the movement that we have built together, and I believe that we’re going to have a good night tonight. Earlier today, folks all over Nevada showed up at community centers, elementary schools, and even casinos to stand in a corner with their neighbors and raise their voices for a person they believe in. **It is the honor of a lifetime that so many of those Nevadans believed in me and in what we’re fighting for, and after today’s caucuses, I am readier than ever before to see this through and put power back into the hands of the people.** When I got into this race, it was because I was worried about what I was seeing in the Democratic primary: a group of Washington insiders promising change, but unlikely to meaningfully reform the very system that keeps them in power. I still feel that way. We can’t fix this broken system of government — a system that puts big corporations and Mar-a-Lago country club members ahead of working families — by putting someone in charge who’s been at the center of that system for nearly 30 years. But now, after seven months on the campaign trail, it’s about more than that. I feel deeply indebted to the million-plus people who have joined our movement. You knocked on doors, donated to help me get on the debate stage, joined our online community, and so much more. Your trust and support is a responsibility I take seriously. I feel honor-bound to do my very best to represent you well in this primary and beat Mr. Trump with you in November. And here’s what I’ll tell you: _I believe that we can win_. Especially after today’s strong showing in Nevada, we are very well-placed to show up big in South Carolina. Our team on the ground is ready to do their part to make that happen. They are knocking on doors, making phone calls, and developing the connections necessary to drive turnout and win the election. But they could really use your help. clean_body: | | | | Friend — I am so incredibly proud of the movement that we have built together, and I believe that we’re going to have a good night tonight. Earlier today, folks all over Nevada showed up at community centers, elementary schools, and even casinos to stand in a corner with their neighbors and raise their voices for a person they believe in. It is the honor of a lifetime that so many of those Nevadans believed in me and in what we’re fighting for, and after today’s caucuses, I am readier than ever before to see this through and put power back into the hands of the people. When I got into this race, it was because I was worried about what I was seeing in the Democratic primary: a group of Washington insiders promising change, but unlikely to meaningfully reform the very system that keeps them in power. I still feel that way. We can’t fix this broken system of government — a system that puts big corporations and Mar-a-Lago country club members ahead of working families — by putting someone in charge who’s been at the center of that system for nearly 30 years. But now, after seven months on the campaign trail, it’s about more than that. I feel deeply indebted to the million-plus people who have joined our movement. You knocked on doors, donated to help me get on the debate stage, joined our online community, and so much more. Your trust and support is a responsibility I take seriously. I feel honor-bound to do my very best to represent you well in this primary and beat Mr. Trump with you in November. And here’s what I’ll tell you: I believe that we can win. Especially after today’s strong showing in Nevada, we are very well-placed to show up big in South Carolina. Our team on the ground is ready to do their part to make that happen. They are knocking on doors, making phone calls, and developing the connections necessary to drive turnout and win the election. But they could really use your help. Thank you for all that you do. I’m grateful for every single person who’s gotten involved in our campaign, and I’m ready to keep this incredible momentum going. Dear Friend, President Trump's administration is repeatedly leveraging provisions of the Federal Vacancies Reform Act to keep "acting" officers in place to avoid Senate confirmation of those appointments. And he has used his authority under the National Emergencies Act to reallocate funds appropriated by Congress for other purposes to build the border wall. While the Constitution gives the legislative branch mechanisms to check the executive branch, the Trump administration is demonstrating that executive power is restrained more by norms than by statutes or Constitutional provisions. If the president faces a legislative branch that rolls over on a regular basis, they can smash these norms and exert enormous power. As your congressman, I would sponsor or co-sponsor legislation to amend both statutes to close the loopholes the administration is leveraging. But the ultimate check on executive power is we, the people. We can express our support for, or opposition to, the new norms this administration is forming - norms that enable an imperial presidency and perpetuate weakened representation of the people - on election day in November. clean_body: With Another Acting DNI | = Dear Friend, President Trump's administration is repeatedly leveraging provisions of the Federal Vacancies Reform Act to keep "acting" officers in place to avoid Senate confirmation of those appointments. And he has used his authority under the National Emergencies Act to reallocate funds appropriated by Congress for other purposes to build the border wall. While the Constitution gives the legislative branch mechanisms to check the executive branch, the Trump administration is demonstrating that executive power is restrained more by norms than by statutes or Constitutional provisions. If the president faces a legislative branch that rolls over on a regular basis, they can smash these norms and exert enormous power. As your congressman, I would sponsor or co-sponsor legislation to amend both statutes to close the loopholes the administration is leveraging. But the ultimate check on executive power is we, the people. We can express our support for, or opposition to, the new norms this administration is forming - norms that enable an imperial presidency and perpetuate weakened representation of the people - on election day in November. Health care, immigration, infrastructure, economic security, income inequality, climate change, criminal justice reform and more are all critical issues we need to address - but we haven't for decades because Congress is divided, dysfunctional, and diminished. =, and put Congress back to work for us. | = | = | = ---|---|--- = | = | = Thank you, Rick | Paid for by Rick Kennedy for Congress Rick Kennedy for Congress 2203 Emmett Pkwy Austin, TX 78726 To unsubscribe, please .

Derek,

**Political campaigns are getting more expensive every year.** In 2014, Virginia Senate candidates spent a combined total of more than **$7 million against Mark Warner** -- and that number is already on the rise in 2020. **[Will you help Mark share his message across Virginia by chipping in today?](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d5ed/53d9897e/5316d2a8/1c89be26/3197328963/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjQ3OCJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-noflipnd?refcode=20200223_f_1_nd__478__&refcode2=ask1&amount=5")**

**We know that the GOP is investing _heavily_ in Virginia this year.** The Trump Victory Initiative hosted over 80 events in the state in 2019, and they continue to pour money into the Commonwealth. Plus, Mark is already facing multiple Republican challengers vying to take his seat in the Senate and undo progress across the Commonwealth. The GOP thinks it's possible to flip Virginia back to red -- it's up to us to show them that they're mistaken.

We've got the grassroots enthusiasm and the numbers to win, but it's crucial that Mark continue to grow his grassroots support network. **Will you donate now to help Mark win re-election in November?**

name: Marguerite Dooley
subject: Volunteering: Dani Brzozowski for Illinois
body:
Good afternoon!

I am reaching out from the Dani Brzozowski for Congress campaign here in
the 16th District, getting in contact with all of our fabulous new and
returning volunteers. Thank you so much for signing up! We are kicking
off our door knocking, phone banking and letter to the editor programs now,
and I would love to plug you in.

*When is a good time in the next week or so for us to meet or jump on the
phone? * I can give you an update on the campaign, learn some more about
your background, and get you started with the volunteer activities you are
most interested in.

We also have a few volunteer events coming up, we would love to see you
there!
Canvass in LaSalle <https://www.facebook.com/events/195562035142852/> -
Sunday 3/1, 12-3pm
Phone bank in Princeton <https://www.facebook.com/events/617192109066739/> -
Tuesday 3/3, 6-8pm

Looking forward to working with you, and talk soon.

--
Marguerite Dooley
Deputy Campaign Manager
Dani for Illinois
c. 802-558-7918

name: Petitions@EugeneForCongress.com
subject: [ACTION REQUESTED]: Help us combat the opioid epidemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10
body:
The opioid epidemic continues to be a dire public health crisis here in Pennsylvania, and it’s time that Congress take action to hold Big Pharma accountable and tackle this epidemic.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15341298/161838478/424986305?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODFmZDhjNC05YjU2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CE9xxlMzWflSGuRNkDgejOC7O-rIJTxBN2jCbcUIAGI=&emci=cd279c68-5b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=e81fd8c4-9b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=10203249) Team,

**The opioid epidemic continues to be a dire public health crisis here in Pennsylvania, and it’s time that Congress take action to hold Big Pharma accountable and tackle this epidemic.**

Treatment and prevention should go hand in hand. In too many instances, addiction begins with legal prescriptions from doctors who were misled about proper use for these medicines. The big pharmaceutical companies who misled medical professionals and profited off of addiction should be held accountable.

[**We need 1,000 grassroots supporters to sign Eugene’s petition calling on Congress to combat the opioid epidemic. Can you be one of them, friend?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15341299/161838479/424986305?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODFmZDhjNC05YjU2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CE9xxlMzWflSGuRNkDgejOC7O-rIJTxBN2jCbcUIAGI=&emci=cd279c68-5b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=e81fd8c4-9b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=10203249) | [End the opioid crisis »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15341300/161838480/424986305?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODFmZDhjNC05YjU2LWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CE9xxlMzWflSGuRNkDgejOC7O-rIJTxBN2jCbcUIAGI=&emci=cd279c68-5b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=e81fd8c4-9b56-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=10203249)
---
**We must focus on addiction as a disease rather than a crime, and address this epidemic in a way that puts people and communities first.**

This issue has impacted hundreds of thousands of Pennsylvanians over the last few years. In 2017 alone, it was estimated that over 300,000 people in Pennsylvania struggled with a drug use disorder -- not to mention the impact it has on the friends and families of those affected.

>From Your Capitol - February 23, 2020

The activity at the capitol increased considerably during the seventh week of this years session as all bills to be considered have been submitted and assigned to a committee. Committee chairmen and vice-chairmen were busy reviewing the bills assigned to their committee and legislators were busy building support for their ideas.

Prisons have been a major consideration this legislative session. Interim Commissioner Tommy Taylor briefed committee members on the issues the Mississippi Department of Corrections (MDOC) is facing and some of the ways they are addressing these problems. One solution Commissioner Taylor informed the committee of was the closing of Unit 29 at the state prison in Parchman, MS. This unit has been allowed by past administrations to deteriorate to a point that the commissioner believes it is impossible to return it to an acceptable standard for use.

House Bill 851 was discussed both in committee and before the House this week. The bill adds an additional member appointed by the governor to the Corrections and Criminal Justice Oversight Task Force. The new member will act as an advocate for offenders and families who have been directly affected by the criminal justice system. The bill passed and has been transmitted to the Senate.

Interstate Compact on Second Amendment Sanctuary

Many have asked about HB 753 – Interstate Compact on Second Amendment Sanctuary. I presented this bill to the Interstate Cooperation Committee this past week but was unable to get the votes to send the bill to the House for consideration. Chairman Tracy Arnold, the primary author of the bill, tabled the bill to give us more time to gather support. The compact is widely supported by Republicans but not by Democrats. Without the support of some of the Democrat members of the committee the bill will not move forward.

clean_body: To read this newsletter in HTML format, click new laws do you think the Mississippi legislature should consider? From Your Capitol - February 23, 2020 The activity at the capitol increased considerably during the seventh week of this years session as all bills to be considered have been submitted and assigned to a committee. Committee chairmen and vice-chairmen were busy reviewing the bills assigned to their committee and legislators were busy building support for their ideas. Prisons have been a major consideration this legislative session. Interim Commissioner Tommy Taylor briefed committee members on the issues the Mississippi Department of Corrections (MDOC) is facing and some of the ways they are addressing these problems. One solution Commissioner Taylor informed the committee of was the closing of Unit 29 at the state prison in Parchman, MS. This unit has been allowed by past administrations to deteriorate to a point that the commissioner believes it is impossible to return it to an acceptable standard for use. House Bill 851 was discussed both in committee and before the House this week. The bill adds an additional member appointed by the governor to the Corrections and Criminal Justice Oversight Task Force. The new member will act as an advocate for offenders and families who have been directly affected by the criminal justice system. The bill passed and has been transmitted to the Senate. Interstate Compact on Second Amendment Sanctuary Many have asked about HB 753 – Interstate Compact on Second Amendment Sanctuary. I presented this bill to the Interstate Cooperation Committee this past week but was unable to get the votes to send the bill to the House for consideration. Chairman Tracy Arnold, the primary author of the bill, tabled the bill to give us more time to gather support. The compact is widely supported by Republicans but not by Democrats. Without the support of some of the Democrat members of the committee the bill will not move forward. Worst Bill (so far) of this Session HB 1017 allowed the city of Bruce to divert funds. . . Friends for Dana Criswell PO Box 1321 Olive Branch, MS 38654 Dana@DanaCriswell.net

Election Day is coming fast but with just a few hours each week, YOU can be the difference and help send someone like your neighbor to Congress.

Hello

Influenza hit our family hard in the last few weeks. But once I knew I wasn't contagious, I was back at it again meeting with Iowans and hearing their concerns.

We need a Congress that works for We the People, ignores the special interests, and gets the job done without all the political bickering.

Together, we can make that difference a reality. With your help, we can send a conservative from outside the political establishment to push for our values in Washington.

Email our campaign TODAY at volunteer@richardsforcongress.com (https://richardsforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ae8e7c784d8480fbbe3b24d82&id=5ebbc50553&e=10eff4eed0&utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489) to get involved. Don't wait until after dinner.

Get your friends involved, like and share our Facebook page, make calls, knock doors, put up signs, and yes make a contribution. Anything you can do to help in your town will be the difference that wins this election ⤵️ (https://emojipedia.org/right-arrow-curving-down/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489)
HOST A GATHERING (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489)
KNOCK DOORS (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489)
LIKE US ON FACEBOOK (http://facebook.com/bretrichards4congress?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489)
PUT UP A SIGN (http://richardsforcongress.com/volunteer?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489)
As always, get in touch with me at bret@richardsforcongress.com (mailto:bret@richardsforcongress.com) or (712)-579-4753 to tell me about your vision for Iowa or to lend a hand on our campaign. I look forward to hearing from you.

Please read on to see what I've been up to this week and all of the things we will accomplish together in the coming months.

I appreciate your continued support.

Your neighbor,
p.s. 99 DAYS until the primary on June 2nd. We NEED your support (http://richardsforcongress.com/contribution?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489) today!
BUILDING OUR NETWORK
Crisscrossed the district again this week collecting the necessary signatures to get our campaign on the ballot. Whether it was Charter Oak, Jefferson, or Laurens, heard from hundreds of Iowans about the need to get more political outsiders in DC and clean up the mess. Also got the chance to show my oldest son around the University of Iowa before he makes some big decisions in his life. I think he'll be a Hawkeye!
RECENT COVERAGE

https://richardsforcongress.com/richards-talks-affirming-life-fixing-healthcare-and-standing-up-to-iran-with-caffeinated-thoughts/?utm_source=Neighborhood&utm_campaign=4e352729ce-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_202012_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_7d99180ded-4e352729ce-25822489

"We have to start sending people like you and me to Congress, not to be career politicians but citizen legislators who will serve for a short time and come home. The Founding Fathers didn’t want people having a career where they find political games to play with our lives."
UP NEXT

Looking forward to getting back to the doors and speaking with everyday Iowans this weekend as well as hearing from young conservatives in Fort Dodge and gathering with local Republicans in Shelby.

STAY INFORMED

Anything you can do to help in your town will be the difference that wins this election ⤵️ () HOST A GATHERING () KNOCK DOORS () LIKE US ON FACEBOOK () PUT UP A SIGN () As always, get in touch with me at bret@richardsforcongress.com (mailto:bret@richardsforcongress.com) or (712)-579-4753 to tell me about your vision for Iowa or to lend a hand on our campaign. I look forward to hearing from you. Please read on to see what I've been up to this week and all of the things we will accomplish together in the coming months. I appreciate your continued support. Your neighbor, p.s. 99 DAYS until the primary on June 2nd. We NEED your support () today! BUILDING OUR NETWORK Crisscrossed the district again this week collecting the necessary signatures to get our campaign on the ballot. Whether it was Charter Oak, Jefferson, or Laurens, heard from hundreds of Iowans about the need to get more political outsiders in DC and clean up the mess. Also got the chance to show my oldest son around the University of Iowa before he makes some big decisions in his life. I think he'll be a Hawkeye! RECENT COVERAGE "We have to start sending people like you and me to Congress, not to be career politicians but citizen legislators who will serve for a short time and come home. The Founding Fathers didn’t want people having a career where they find political games to play with our lives." UP NEXT Looking forward to getting back to the doors and speaking with everyday Iowans this weekend as well as hearing from young conservatives in Fort Dodge and gathering with local Republicans in Shelby.
